compiled_state.title: CI-enforced quality gates: staged CI pipeline with LLM review and GitHub Ruleset enforcement
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Context

DSO plugin users — primarily AI agents running in Claude Code worktree sessions, and secondarily human developers using the plugin — face a commit workflow enforced by local pre-commit hooks and PreToolUse hooks (test gate, review gate, lint, format). For agents, test suites hit Claude Code's ~73s Bash timeout ceiling, requiring elaborate workarounds. For both agents and humans, local enforcement lives alongside the actor it's guarding, requiring elaborate safeguards (Layer 2 bypass detection, diff hash verification, reviewer-findings.json integrity) that add friction to every commit. Shifting enforcement to CI via GitHub Rulesets and required status checks makes the enforcement independent of the local actor, eliminates timeout issues for tests, and creates the foundation for removing local review/test gate hooks.

A 2026-04-26 retrospective (docs/proposed-mitigations-4-26.md) folded three additional defense-in-depth gates into this epic's required-check surface: a PR-level full-suite validation (G7-M3), platform-matrix promotion to a pre-merge gate (G8-M3), and a Linux-strict pipefail test variant (G12-M3). Each is an additive required-check dimension on the Ruleset; together they close gaps where the per-file pre-commit gate and pre-release gate cannot reach.

## Success Criteria

- After onboarding completes, direct pushes to main are blocked — all changes must go through a pull request (the onboarding process itself may use an initial direct push to install the CI workflow before activating the Ruleset)
- PRs require all CI status checks to pass before merging: lint, format, tests, and LLM-based code review; the LLM review check passes when the review completes with no findings at severity critical — it exits non-zero otherwise
- CI runs checks as separate GitHub Actions jobs with needs: dependencies: a lint/format job, a tests job (needs lint), and an LLM review job (needs tests); expensive jobs only run when prior jobs pass
- The LLM review CI job reads API keys from GitHub Secrets and model IDs from GitHub Variables; each of the 3 tiers (light, standard, frontier) has an independently configurable model ID variable; provider is hardcoded to Anthropic in this epic, with the variable naming convention supporting future provider additions
- The /dso:onboarding skill configures the GitHub repository: (a) merges the CI workflow file to main first, (b) then creates the Ruleset via gh api POST with --input JSON, (c) with pre-flight checks for gh version and admin permissions — emitting a manual setup guide and continuing without the Ruleset if either check fails; (d) enables auto-merge on the repository
- For the initial implementation, Anthropic/Claude is the only available LLM provider; the LLM review job includes a configurable timeout (default 10 minutes) and an admin Ruleset bypass actor (RepositoryRole actor_id 5) so PRs can still merge during provider outages
- An E2E validation test exercises the full PR lifecycle: creates a test branch, pushes a change, creates a PR, verifies CI status checks appear, and asserts the PR cannot merge until checks pass and merges successfully after checks pass
- A `validate.sh --ci` job runs in the CI pipeline as a separate GitHub Actions job (needs: tests; runs in parallel with the LLM review job) and is included in the Ruleset's required-check list; the job passes only when `validate.sh --ci` exits 0
- `ticket-platform-matrix.yml` fires on every PR (path filter removed or broadened to any change under `plugins/dso/scripts/` or `plugins/dso/hooks/`); its three matrix legs (`linux-bash4`, `macos-bash3`, `alpine-busybox`) are each individually included in the Ruleset's required-check list
- A Linux-only test-job variant runs the test suite with `BASH_OPTS='-eo pipefail'` exported, is included in the Ruleset's required-check list, and surfaces SIGPIPE-class failures that macOS local development cannot reproduce

## Spike Findings

All spike objectives validated on 2026-04-06 against a fresh public repo (JoeOakhartNava/dso-spike-ci-rulesets):
- gh api POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/rulesets with --input ruleset.json works on first try
- Check context strings accepted on fresh repo without prior CI runs (do_not_enforce_on_create not needed)
- Direct push blocked with clear error message
- needs: chain works correctly (lint -> tests -> LLM review)
- mergeStateStatus: BLOCKED while checks pending, CLEAN after pass
- gh pr merge --squash works after checks pass
- gh pr merge --auto queues merge and merges automatically after checks pass
- Bypass actor (RepositoryRole actor_id: 5) added via PATCH successfully
- Default branch detection: gh repo view --json defaultBranchRef works
- Rulesets require GitHub Pro for private repos; public repos work on Free plan
- Auto-merge requires gh repo edit --enable-auto-merge before gh pr merge --auto works

## Dependencies

- 6add-c1fc (Integrate Semgrep into CI) should be sequenced after this epic (extends the base CI pipeline)
- Environmental: gh CLI with admin scope on target repos, Anthropic API credentials as GitHub Secrets
- Rulesets require GitHub Pro plan for private repos (or public repo on Free plan)

## Approach

GitHub Rulesets + staged CI pipeline with needs:-chained jobs (lint/format -> tests -> LLM review), plus a `validate.sh --ci` job (needs: tests, parallel with LLM review) and a Linux-only pipefail-strict test variant. The `ticket-platform-matrix.yml` workflow's path-filtered PR trigger is broadened so its three platform legs become pre-merge required checks. Onboarding configures the Ruleset via gh api POST --input ruleset.json. Bootstrap sequence: onboarding merges the CI workflow file to main via direct push before Ruleset creation, then creates the Ruleset with the full required-check list (lint, format, tests, LLM review, validate-ci, platform-matrix legs x3, pipefail-strict). Default branch detected dynamically via gh repo view --json defaultBranchRef. Anthropic's claude-code-action is the production-ready option for LLM review in CI.

## Re-decomposition Plan (2026-04-26 scope expansion)

The epic was decomposed into 6 stories (d025-87d4, fc8b-aab0, bea2-bd5e, a592-0992, 5f8f-ddac, 7601-5dfa) before the 2026-04-26 scope expansion. The new SCs (validate.sh --ci, platform-matrix promotion, Linux-strict pipefail) are absorbed as follows; `/dso:preplanning` should re-decompose:

- `d025-87d4` (CI workflow) — edit: add `validate.sh --ci` job (needs: tests, parallel with LLM review) to the staged chain. Done definition gains: "validate-ci job is a separate Actions job with needs: tests, exits non-zero when validate.sh --ci fails."
- `fc8b-aab0` (Ruleset provisioning) — edit: required-check list expands to include validate-ci, ticket-platform-matrix legs (linux-bash4, macos-bash3, alpine-busybox), and pipefail-strict. Done definition gains: "Ruleset required-check list covers all 6 base checks plus 5 expansion checks (11 total)."
- NEW story (G8-M3) — Platform matrix promotion to required PR check: remove or broaden the path filter on `ticket-platform-matrix.yml`'s pull_request trigger so it fires on any change under `plugins/dso/scripts/` or `plugins/dso/hooks/`; verify all 3 legs fire on a no-op PR. Blocked by `d025-87d4` for ordering only.
- NEW story (G12-M3) — Linux-strict pipefail test job: add a Linux-only CI job that exports `BASH_OPTS='-eo pipefail'` and runs the test suite, surfacing SIGPIPE-class failures invisible on macOS local development. Blocked by `d025-87d4`.
- `a592-0992` (Onboarding) — unchanged; the Ruleset created by `fc8b-aab0` already includes the expanded check list.
- `5f8f-ddac` (E2E test) — minor edit: E2E test verifies all required checks (11 total) appear on PR and block merge until green.
- `7601-5dfa` (Docs) — unchanged; already covers "all".

## Scenario Analysis

- Anthropic API outage blocks all PRs (critical): mitigated by admin bypass actor in Ruleset and configurable job timeout
- gh token lacks admin scope (critical): onboarding pre-flight validates permissions; emits manual guide on failure
- Wrong branch pattern in Ruleset (critical): onboarding detects default branch dynamically
- LLM review timeout (high): job has explicit timeout-minutes
- Unset GitHub Variables (high): CI job validates required variables at start with clear diagnostic
- Invalid model IDs (high): CI job surfaces model ID in error with documentation link
- Stale API key during rotation (high): CI job surfaces 401-specific error pointing to Secrets rotation
- validate.sh --ci flake masks real failure (medium): mitigated by hermetic env block (G9-M1) and explicit unset of CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT in CI step
- Platform-matrix leg flake blocks merge (medium): legs are deterministic suites; flake should file a P1 ticket per G8-M1 policy rather than be tolerated
- pipefail-strict job fails on legitimate macOS-only behaviour (low): job is Linux-only by design; macOS-specific tests run in their own matrix leg

### Planning Intelligence Log

- **Web research (Step 2.6)**: triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: external integration (GitHub Rulesets API, gh CLI, GitHub Actions, Anthropic API, claude-code-action), security/credentials (GitHub Secrets, Variables, bypass actor permissions)
- **Scenario analysis (Step 2.75)**: triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 7 (initial) + 3 (2026-04-26 expansion) = 10
- **Practitioner-requested additional cycles**: none
- **Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0)**: not triggered
- **Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5)**: triggered — resolved via live spike on 2026-04-06 against fresh public repo (JoeOakhartNava/dso-spike-ci-rulesets) confirming all 11 CI behaviors
- **LLM-instruction signal (Step 5)**: triggered — keyword: skill file modifications (onboarding SKILL.md repo-configuration additions for CI workflow + Ruleset provisioning)
- **Scale context (Step 0)**: small scale (default) — per-PR / per-onboarding invocation; not volume-sensitive
- **Scope expansion (2026-04-26)**: G7-M3, G8-M3, G12-M3 absorbed from docs/proposed-mitigations-4-26.md; SC8/SC9/SC10 added; re-decomposition plan recorded above

comments.body: Adversarial review: 9 findings, 6 accepted. Red team: opus. Blue team: sonnet. Rejected: ci.yml collision (template, not replacement), local review coexistence (intentional), enforcement.mode toggle (Epic B scope).
comments.author: Test

comments.body:
Scope expansion (2026-04-26, from docs/proposed-mitigations-4-26.md):

Three additional mitigations absorbed into this epic's CI gates / Ruleset enforcement scope:

- **G7-M3** — Add 'validate.sh --ci passes' as a required CI status check in the Ruleset (alongside lint, format, tests, LLM review). PR cannot merge until validate.sh --ci is green.
- **G8-M3** — Promote ticket-platform-matrix.yml to a required PR check by removing its current path-filtered pull_request trigger (or replacing it with a broader filter that fires on any plugins/dso/scripts/ or hooks/ change). Matrix legs (linux-bash4, macos-bash3, alpine-busybox) become pre-merge gates.
- **G12-M3** — Add a Linux-only test-job variant with BASH_OPTS='-eo pipefail' exported to surface SIGPIPE divergences that macOS local development cannot reproduce. New CI job in the staged needs:-chain.

Rationale: this epic establishes the CI-enforced quality gate and Ruleset structure. Each addition is a new required-check dimension on the same surface. G7-M3 and G8-M3 expand the Ruleset's required-check list; G12-M3 adds a new test-job sibling. All three are additive and do not conflict with the existing 6-job pipeline structure.

Children may need re-decomposition once SCs are amended (currently 6 stories: d025-87d4, fc8b-aab0, bea2-bd5e, a592-0992, 5f8f-ddac, 7601-5dfa). Likely affects d025-87d4 (CI workflow) and a592-0992 (Ruleset config).
comments.author: Test
